title: ONE Business Credo and Code of Conduct
initial_excerpt:
By
ONE Acticulate
ONE Business Credo and Code of Conduct
START COURSE

Welcome to the ONE Business Credo and Code of Conduct E-learning Course!

In this course, you will discover the core beliefs and principles that shape ONE’s culture and guide our daily actions.

Course Introduction

ONE Business Credo

Code of Conduct Introduction

2. Safeguard our Environment, Safety and Quality
3. Observe Ethical Conduct and Compliance with Laws and Regulations
4. Nurture an Inclusive Workplace and Respect for Human Rights
5. Enhance Information Security And Protection Of Assets

Lesson 3 - Code of Conduct Introduction
1. Act as ONE

Chapter 2

Code of Conduct

1. Act As ONE

Responsibility & General Standards

The Code of Conduct is a collective commitment. While leadership sets the tone, every employee at ONE is responsible for maintaining our unified and safe culture. This section explores the specific roles leadership and employees play in safeguarding our values.

Click on each role to see responsibility and general standards of behaviour at ONE:

Top Management

Tone from the Top: Top management act with integrity, showing the commitment in achieving the spirit and ideals set forth in this Code.

Enforcement: Take any violations of this Code seriously, hold any ONE Employees who do not comply with this Code accountable, regardless of position.

Accountability: Accountable not just for what ONE employees do, but also for the actions of ONE's business partners.

Employees do what leaders do, not just what leaders say.

ONE Employees

KNOWLEDGE CHECK

Bad Example

It is the end of the financial year, and Sarah has a backlog of payments to process. She feels frustrated that each payment requires approval, as it is inconvenient and slows her down. She decides to bypass the approval process to clear her backlog.

CONTINUE
Go to Section 2 of the Code

KNOWLEDGE CHECK

Is this compliant with ONE's Code of Conduct?

Yes

Try Again!

(Scroll down to read more)

Sarah’s decision to bypass the approval process violates core principles of the Code of Conduct.

Accuracy of Corporate Records: By skipping the authorized approval, the record no longer "properly reflects the true state of business."

Deliberate Misstatement: Using a system workaround to mark something as "Approved" when it hasn't been reviewed is a form of falsification.

Wilful Misconduct: Knowingly ignoring internal procedure, regardless of the reason, is prohibited.

TRY AGAIN 
Go to Section 2 of the Code
